metadata.note: Data value types (DV_*) are not included in the provided Common IM PDF. They are part of a separate Data Types specification document not included in this extraction.

composition.name: COMPOSITION
composition.description: Content of one version in a VERSIONED_COMPOSITION. A Composition is considered the unit of modification of the record, the unit of transmission in record Extracts, and the unit of attestation by authorising clinicians.
composition.when_to_use: Use as the primary container for clinical information within an EHR. Recommended to populate the inherited uid attribute with the object_id from the enclosing VERSION object.

attributes.name: language
attributes.description: Mandatory indicator of the localised language in which this Composition is written. Coded from openEHR Code Set languages.
attributes.notes: Language of an Entry if different from the Composition is indicated in ENTRY.language

attributes.name: territory
attributes.description: Name of territory in which this Composition was written. Coded from openEHR countries code set (ISO 3166 standard).

attributes.name: category
attributes.description: Temporal category of this Composition: 431|persistent| (potential life-time validity), 451|episodic| (valid over the life of a care episode), or 433|event| (valid at the time of recording).
attributes.notes: Coded from openEHR 'category' group

attributes.name: context
attributes.description: The clinical session context of this Composition, i.e. the contextual attributes of the clinical session.

attributes.name: composer
attributes.description: The person primarily responsible for the content of the Composition. May be the patient (using PARTY_SELF instance).
attributes.notes: This is the identifier which should appear on the screen

attributes.name: content
attributes.description: The content of this Composition.

functions.name: is_persistent
functions.description: True if category is 431|persistent|, False otherwise. Useful for finding Compositions guaranteed to be of interest to most users.

invariants: Content_valid: content /= Void implies not content.is_empty

event_context.name: EVENT_CONTEXT
event_context.description: Documents the context information of a healthcare event involving the subject of care and the health system. Healthcare events include patient contacts and any other business activity such as pathology investigations.
event_context.when_to_use: Use to record contextual information about clinical sessions and healthcare events. The context information recorded here is independent of the version audit attributes.

attributes.name: start_time
attributes.description: Start time of the clinical session or other kind of event during which a provider performs a service for the patient.

attributes.name: end_time
attributes.description: Optional end time of the clinical session.

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: The actual location where the session occurred, e.g. 'microbiology lab 2', 'home', 'ward A3'.

attributes.name: setting
attributes.description: The setting in which the clinical session took place. Coded using the openEHR Terminology, setting group.

attributes.name: other_context
attributes.description: Other optional context which will be archetyped.

attributes.name: health_care_facility
attributes.description: The health care facility under whose care the event took place. The most specific workgroup or delivery unit within a care delivery enterprise.
attributes.notes: Used to ensure medico-legal accountability

attributes.name: participations
attributes.description: Parties involved in the healthcare event, typically including physicians and sometimes the patient.

invariants: Participations_validity: participations /= Void implies not participations.is_empty
location_valid: location /= Void implies not location.is_empty

entry.name: ENTRY
entry.description: Abstract parent of all ENTRY subtypes. An ENTRY is the root of a logical item of hard clinical information created in the clinical statement context, within a clinical session.
entry.when_to_use: Use as the base class for all clinical entries. ENTRY is the minimal unit of information any query should return.

attributes.name: language
attributes.description: Mandatory indicator of the localised language in which this Entry is written. Coded from openEHR Code Set languages.

attributes.name: encoding
attributes.description: Name of character set in which text values in this Entry are encoded. Coded from openEHR Code Set character sets.

attributes.name: other_participations
attributes.description: Other participations at ENTRY level.

attributes.name: workflow_id
attributes.description: Identifier of externally held workflow engine data for this workflow execution.

attributes.name: subject
attributes.description: Id of human subject of this ENTRY (organ donor, foetus, family member, or other clinically relevant person).

attributes.name: provider
attributes.description: Optional identification of provider of the information in this ENTRY (patient, patient agent, clinician, device or software).
attributes.notes: Generally only used when the recorder needs to make it explicit

functions.name: subject_is_self
functions.description: Returns True if this Entry is about the subject of the EHR (subject attribute is of type PARTY_SELF).

invariants: Other_participations_valid: other_participations /= Void implies not other_participations.is_empty

admin_entry.name: ADMIN_ENTRY
admin_entry.description: Entry subtype for administrative information, i.e. information about setting up the clinical process, but not itself clinically relevant.
admin_entry.when_to_use: Use for administrative details of admission, episode, ward location, discharge, and appointment information. Not to be used for any clinically significant information.

attributes.name: data
attributes.description: Content of the Admin Entry. The data of the Entry; modelled in archetypes.

care_entry.name: CARE_ENTRY
care_entry.description: Abstract parent of all clinical ENTRY subtypes. A CARE_ENTRY defines protocol and guideline attributes for all clinical Entry subtypes.
care_entry.when_to_use: Use as the base class for all clinically significant entries (OBSERVATION, EVALUATION, INSTRUCTION, ACTION).

attributes.name: protocol
attributes.description: Description of the method (how) the information in this entry was arrived at. For OBSERVATIONs, description of method or instrument. For EVALUATIONs, how the evaluation was arrived at. For INSTRUCTIONs, how to execute.
attributes.notes: May take the form of references to guidelines, knowledge references, or clinical reasons

attributes.name: guideline_id
attributes.description: Optional external identifier of guideline creating this Entry if relevant.

observation.name: OBSERVATION
observation.description: Entry subtype for all clinical data in the past or present. OBSERVATION data is expressed using HISTORY<T>, guaranteeing it is situated in time. Used for all notionally objective (measured) observations and patient-reported phenomena.
observation.when_to_use: Use for recording measured or observed data. Not for opinions, future statements, instructions, intentions, or plans.

attributes.name: data
attributes.description: The data of this observation, in the form of a history of values which may be of any complexity.

attributes.name: state
attributes.description: Optional recording of the state of subject during the observation process, in the form of a separate history of values.
attributes.notes: State may also be recorded within the History of the data attribute

evaluation.name: EVALUATION
evaluation.description: Entry type for evaluation statements. Used for all kinds of statements which evaluate other information, such as interpretations, diagnoses, differential diagnoses, hypotheses, risk assessments, goals and plans.
evaluation.when_to_use: Use for evaluation and interpretation of information. Should not be used for actionable statements such as medication orders (use INSTRUCTION instead).

attributes.name: data
attributes.description: The data of this evaluation, in the form of a spatial data structure.

instruction.name: INSTRUCTION
instruction.description: Used to specify actions in the future. Enables simple and complex specifications to be expressed, including in a fully-computable workflow form. Used for any actionable statement such as medication and therapeutic orders, monitoring, recall and review.
instruction.when_to_use: Use for any actionable statement (medication orders, therapeutic orders, monitoring). Enough details must be provided for the specification to be directly executed by an actor.

attributes.name: narrative
attributes.description: Mandatory human-readable version of what the Instruction is about.

attributes.name: expiry_time
attributes.description: Optional expiry date/time to assist determination of when an Instruction can be assumed to have expired.
attributes.notes: Helps prevent false listing of Instructions as Active

attributes.name: wf_definition
attributes.description: Optional workflow engine executable expression of the Instruction.

attributes.name: activities
attributes.description: List of all activities in Instruction.

invariants: Activities_valid: activities /= Void implies not activities.is_empty

activity.name: ACTIVITY
activity.description: Defines a single activity within an Instruction, such as a medication administration.
activity.when_to_use: Use to specify individual activities that make up the larger Instruction. Each Activity corresponds to a single ActionTypical example is a medication administration.

attributes.name: timing
attributes.description: Timing of the activity, in the form of a parsable string. Preferred syntax is ISO8601 'R' format, but other formats may be used including HL7 GTS.
attributes.notes: May be omitted if timing is represented structurally or unavailable (legacy data)

attributes.name: action_archetype_id
attributes.description: Perl-compliant regular expression pattern indicating the valid identifiers of archetypes for Actions corresponding to this Activity specification.
attributes.notes: Defaults to /.*/,meaning any archetype

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Description of the activity, in the form of an archetyped structure.

action.name: ACTION
action.description: Used to record a clinical action that has been performed, which may have been ad hoc or due to the execution of an Activity in an Instruction workflow. Every Action corresponds to a careflow step.
action.when_to_use: Use to record that an action has been performed. Actions may be performed ad hoc or as a result of executing an Instruction. Use to document careflow steps and state machine transitions.

attributes.name: time
attributes.description: Point in time at which this action completed.

attributes.name: ism_transition
attributes.description: Details of transition in the Instruction state machine caused by this Action.

attributes.name: instruction_details
attributes.description: Details of the Instruction that caused this Action to be performed, if there was one.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Description of the action that has been performed, in the form of an archetyped structure.

instruction_details.name: INSTRUCTION_DETAILS
instruction_details.description: Used to record details of the Instruction causing an Action.
instruction_details.when_to_use: Use within ACTION to reference the source Instruction and specify which Activity within that Instruction caused the Action to be performed.

attributes.name: instruction_id
attributes.description: Reference to causing Instruction.

attributes.name: activity_id
attributes.description: Identifier of Activity within Instruction, in the form of its archetype path.

attributes.name: wf_details
attributes.description: Various workflow engine state details, potentially including condition that fired, notifications, and other workflow engine state.
attributes.notes: Structure and semantics not currently defined

ism_transition.name: ISM_TRANSITION
ism_transition.description: Model of a transition in the Instruction State Machine (ISM), caused by a careflow step. The attributes document the careflow step as well as the ISM transition.
ism_transition.when_to_use: Use within ACTION to record the state machine transition and careflow step that resulted from executing an action in response to an Instruction.

attributes.name: current_state
attributes.description: The ISM current state. Coded by openEHR terminology group Instruction states.

attributes.name: transition
attributes.description: The ISM transition which occurred to arrive in the current_state. Coded by openEHR terminology group Instruction transitions.

attributes.name: careflow_step
attributes.description: The step in the careflow process which occurred as part of generating this action (e.g. 'dispense', 'start_administration'). Represents the clinical label for the activity.
attributes.notes: Defined in archetype; represents clinical form vs. current_state which represents ISM computable form

attributes.name: reason
attributes.description: Optional possibility of adding one or more reasons for this careflow step having been taken.
attributes.notes: Multiple reasons may occur in medication management

invariants: Transition_valid: transition /= Void implies terminology (Terminology_id_openehr).has_code_for_group_id (Group_id_instruction_transitions, transition.defining_code)

pathable.name: PATHABLE
pathable.description: The PATHABLE class defines the pathing capabilities used by nearly all classes in the openEHR reference model. PATHABLE objects can locate child objects using paths and know their parent object in a compositional hierarchy.
pathable.when_to_use: Use as the base class for hierarchical data structures. PATHABLE provides the infrastructure for navigating archetyped structures using paths.

functions.name: parent
functions.description: Parent of this node in a compositional hierarchy.

functions.name: item_at_path
functions.description: The item at a path (relative to this item); only valid for unique paths.

functions.name: items_at_path
functions.description: List of items corresponding to a non-unique path.

functions.name: path_exists
functions.description: True if the path exists in the data with respect to the current item.

functions.name: path_unique
functions.description: True if the path corresponds to a single item in the data.

functions.name: path_of_item
functions.description: The path to an item relative to the root of this archetyped structure.

locatable.name: LOCATABLE
locatable.description: Root class of all information model classes that can be archetyped. Most classes in the openEHR reference model inherit from LOCATABLE, which defines the idea of locatability in an archetyped structure.
locatable.when_to_use: Use as the base class for all archetyped content. LOCATABLE provides runtime naming and archetype node identification.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Runtime name of this fragment, used to build runtime paths. The term provided via a clinical application to name this EHR construct.
attributes.notes: Retention in the EHR faithfully preserves the original label by which this entry was known to end users

attributes.name: archetype_node_id
attributes.description: Design-time archetype identifier of this node taken from its generating archetype; used to build archetype paths. Always in the form of an at-code (e.g., at0005).
attributes.notes: At archetype root, value is always stringified form of archetype_id from archetype_details

attributes.name: uid
attributes.description: Optional globally unique object identifier for root points of archetyped structures.

attributes.name: links
attributes.description: Links to other archetyped structures (data whose root object inherits from ARCHETYPED, such as ENTRY, SECTION).
attributes.notes: Links may be to structures in other compositions

attributes.name: archetype_details
attributes.description: Details of archetyping used on this node.

attributes.name: feeder_audit
attributes.description: Audit trail from non-openEHR system of original commit of information, or from a conversion gateway.

functions.name: concept
functions.description: Clinical concept of the archetype as a whole (derived from the archetype_node_id of the root node).

functions.name: is_archetype_root
functions.description: True if this node is the root of an archetyped structure.

invariants: Links_valid: links /= Void implies not links.is_empty

archetyped.name: ARCHETYPED
archetyped.description: Archetypes act as the configuration basis for the particular structures of instances defined by the reference model. An instance of ARCHETYPED contains the relevant archetype identification information.
archetyped.when_to_use: Use to document the archetype and template information used to create an archetyped data structure.

attributes.name: archetype_id
attributes.description: Globally unique archetype identifier.

attributes.name: template_id
attributes.description: Globally unique template identifier, if a template was active at this point in the structure.
attributes.notes: Normally used at the top of a top-level structure, but templates may be used at lower levels

attributes.name: rm_version
attributes.description: Version of the openEHR reference model used to create this object (e.g., 1.0, 1.2.4).

link.name: LINK
link.description: The LINK type defines a logical relationship between two items, such as two ENTRYs or an ENTRY and a COMPOSITION. Links can be used across compositions and across EHRs.
link.when_to_use: Use to represent logical relationships between archetyped structures. Links should be between archetyped structures only (whole ENTRYs, SECTIONs, COMPOSITIONs).

attributes.name: meaning
attributes.description: Used to describe the relationship, usually in clinical terms (e.g., 'in response to', 'follow-up to').
attributes.notes: Categories include generic, documenting, reporting, organisational, clinical, circumstancial, and view management

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: The type attribute indicates a clinical or domain-level meaning (e.g., 'problem', 'issue').
attributes.notes: Can be used to categorize which links must be followed or can be broken when extracts are created

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: The logical target object in the link relation.

feeder_audit.name: FEEDER_AUDIT
feeder_audit.description: Defines the semantics of an audit trail which is constructed to describe the origin of data that have been transformed into openEHR form and committed to the system.
feeder_audit.when_to_use: Use to document the origin and transformation of data from non-openEHR systems. Provides traceability for data conversion and integration.

attributes.name: originating_system_item_ids
attributes.description: Identifiers used for the item in the originating system (e.g., filler and placer ids).

attributes.name: feeder_system_item_ids
attributes.description: Identifiers used for the item in the feeder system, where distinct from the originating system.

attributes.name: original_content
attributes.description: Optional inline inclusion of or reference to original content corresponding to the openEHR content. Typically a URI reference to a document or message.

attributes.name: originating_system_audit
attributes.description: Audit information for the information item from the originating system.

attributes.name: feeder_system_audit
attributes.description: Audit information for the information item from the feeder system, if different from originating system.

feeder_audit_details.name: FEEDER_AUDIT_DETAILS
feeder_audit_details.description: Audit details for any system in a feeder system chain. Represents the general notion of who/where/when the information item was created. None of the attributes is mandatory, though various combinations will usually be mandatory in different scenarios.
feeder_audit_details.when_to_use: Use to document audit information from legacy systems or conversion gateways. Provides the where/when/who context for data transformation.

attributes.name: system_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the system which handled the information item. The IT system owned by the organisation legally responsible.

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: Identifier of the particular site/facility within an organisation which handled the item.
attributes.notes: For computability, needs to be a PKI identifier in the PARTY_IDENTIFIED identifier list

attributes.name: subject
attributes.description: Identifiers for subject of the received information item.

attributes.name: provider
attributes.description: Provider(s) who created, committed, forwarded or otherwise handled the item.

attributes.name: time
attributes.description: Time of handling the item. For originating system, time of creation; for feeder system, time of accession or handling.

attributes.name: version_id
attributes.description: Any identifier used in the system such as 'interim', 'final', or numeric versions if available.

party_proxy.name: PARTY_PROXY
party_proxy.description: Abstract concept of a proxy description of a party, including an optional link to data for this party in a demographic or other identity management system.
party_proxy.when_to_use: Use as the base class for party references in the EHR. Subtyped into PARTY_IDENTIFIED and PARTY_SELF.

attributes.name: external_ref
attributes.description: Optional reference to more detailed demographic or identification information for this party in an external system.

party_self.name: PARTY_SELF
party_self.description: Party proxy representing the subject of the record. Used to indicate that the party is the owner of the record.
party_self.when_to_use: Use to represent the patient or subject of the EHR. May or may not have external_ref set.

party_identified.name: PARTY_IDENTIFIED
party_identified.description: Proxy data for an identified party other than the subject of the record, minimally consisting of identifiers and optional link to external data. There must be at least one of name, identifier or external_ref present.
party_identified.when_to_use: Use to describe parties where only identifiers may be known (e.g., healthcare providers, institutions). Should not be used to include patient identifying information.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Optional human-readable name in String form.

attributes.name: identifiers
attributes.description: One or more formal identifiers (possibly computable).

invariants: Basic_validity: name /= Void or identifiers /= Void or external_ref /= Void
Name_valid: name /= Void implies not name.is_empty
Identifiers_valid: identifiers /= Void implies not identifiers.is_empty

party_related.name: PARTY_RELATED
party_related.description: Proxy type for identifying a party and its relationship to the subject of the record. Use where the relationship between the party and the subject must be known.
party_related.when_to_use: Use when recording participations where the relationship to the subject is clinically important (e.g., parent of child patient).

attributes.name: relationship
attributes.description: Relationship of subject of this ENTRY to the subject of the record. May be coded. If the party is the patient, coded as 'self'.

participation.name: PARTICIPATION
participation.description: Model of a participation of a Party (any Actor or Role) in an activity. Used to represent any participation of a Party in an activity not explicitly in the model (e.g., assisting nurse). Can record past or future participations.
participation.when_to_use: Use to record any participation of a party in a healthcare activity. Should not be used in place of more permanent relationships between demographic entities.

attributes.name: function
attributes.description: The function of the Party in this participation. A given party might participate in more than one way in a particular activity.
attributes.notes: Should be coded but cannot be limited to HL7v3:ParticipationFunction vocabulary

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description: Optional field for the 'mode' of the performer/activity interaction (e.g., present, by telephone, by email).

attributes.name: performer
attributes.description: The id and possibly demographic system link of the party participating in the activity.

attributes.name: time
attributes.description: The time interval during which the participation took place (observational context) or intended time (future contexts).

invariants: Mode_valid: mode /= Void implies terminology (Terminology_id_openehr).has_code_for_group_id (Group_id_participation_mode, mode.defining_code)

audit_details.name: AUDIT_DETAILS
audit_details.description: The set of attributes required to document the committal of an information item to a repository.
audit_details.when_to_use: Use to document system-level audit information for all changes committed to the EHR system.

attributes.name: system_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the logical EHR system where the change was committed. Owned by the organisation legally responsible for the EHR.
attributes.notes: Distinct from any application or hosting infrastructure

attributes.name: time_committed
attributes.description: Time of committal of the item.

attributes.name: change_type
attributes.description: Type of change. Coded using the openEHR Terminology audit change type group.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Reason for committal. May qualify the change_type value (e.g., 'Folder added').

attributes.name: committer
attributes.description: Identity and optional reference into identity management service of user who committed the item.

attestation.name: ATTESTATION
attestation.description: Record an attestation of a party (the committer) to item(s) of record content. An attestation is an explicit signing by one healthcare agent of particular content for various purposes (authorization, witnessing, acknowledgement).
attestation.when_to_use: Use to document explicit digital attestations (signatures) by healthcare professionals. Common use cases: authorization of controlled substances, witnessing by senior clinician, acknowledgement by intended recipient.

attributes.name: attested_view
attributes.description: Optional visual representation of content attested (e.g., screen image).

attributes.name: proof
attributes.description: Proof of attestation.

attributes.name: items
attributes.description: Items attested, expressed as fully qualified runtime paths. May include fine-grained items from other systems (not recommended).
attributes.notes: Otherwise assumed to be for the entire VERSION

attributes.name: reason
attributes.description: Reason of this attestation. Optionally coded by openEHR Terminology (values: authorization, witness, etc.).

attributes.name: is_pending
attributes.description: True if this attestation is outstanding; False if completed.

invariants: Items_valid: items /= Void implies not items.is_empty
